title: banksia integrifolia subsp. monticola
intro: Banksia integrifolia subsp. monticola, also known as the towering banksia, is a subspecies of the Banksia integrifolia plant, which is a flowering plant native to eastern Australia. The towering banksia has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and protect the skin from infections and irritation. The plant has been used to promote health and well-being, with its leaves and flowers said to have antioxidant and anti-arthritic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve joint health. The towering banksia can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although it should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
